1102.name: The audit log was cleared
1102.notable: Anti-forensics — someone cleared the Security log

4624.name: An account was successfully logged on

4625.name: An account failed to log on
4625.notable: Brute-force / password-spray — check volume, LogonType, and source address

4648.name: A logon was attempted using explicit credentials
4648.notable: runas / lateral movement with alternate credentials

4662.name: An operation was performed on an object

4672.name: Special privileges assigned to new logon

4688.name: A new process has been created
4688.notable: Process creation — check CommandLine (if enabled) and parent process

4697.name: A service was installed in the system
4697.notable: Persistence / lateral movement (e.g., PsExec, malicious service)

4724.name: An attempt was made to reset an account's password

4728.name: A member was added to a security-enabled global group
4728.notable: Privilege escalation (e.g., added to Domain Admins)

4732.name: A member was added to a security-enabled local group

4740.name: A user account was locked out
4740.notable: Often follows a burst of 4625 failed logons

4768.name: A Kerberos authentication ticket (TGT) was requested
4768.notable: AS-REQ — watch for AS-REP roasting (no pre-auth)

4769.name: A Kerberos service ticket was requested
4769.notable: Kerberoasting — RC4 (0x17) tickets and high volume

4776.name: The domain controller attempted to validate credentials

4798.name: A user's local group membership was enumerated

4799.name: A security-enabled local group membership was enumerated

5140.name: A network share object was accessed

5145.name: A network share object was checked for access
5145.notable: Lateral movement / share enumeration (e.g., ADMIN$, C$)

7045.name: A new service was installed
7045.notable: PsExec / service-based persistence or lateral movement

1.name: Sysmon: Process creation
1.notable: Full CommandLine, hashes, and parent — high-value telemetry

3.name: Sysmon: Network connection
3.notable: Maps a process to its network destinations

7.name: Sysmon: Image loaded
7.notable: DLL loads — useful for DLL sideloading detection

12.name: Sysmon: Registry object added or deleted
